title: Duck boat overturns in Cambridge, injuring 11 people
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Eleven people were injured when a duck boat overturned on a ramp along the Charles River in Cambridge.
The vehicle was being towed after a mechanical issue when the tow rope snapped, causing it to flip.
Boston Duck Tours operations were suspended following the incident; authorities are investigating with the U.S. Coast Guard.

title: Why JJ Peterka was worth the price of 2 1st-round picks for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins traded two first-round picks to acquire winger JJ Peterka from the Utah Mammoth.
Peterka, 24, has averaged 26 goals per season over the last three years with Buffalo and Utah.
Peterka's speed and potential for 30 goals will address Boston's need for scoring punch and speed.

title: MassHealth will stop covering weight-loss drugs next week
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: MassHealth will stop covering weight-loss drugs like Wegovy and Zepbound for 22,000 members starting July 1.
The policy change, part of Massachusetts' fiscal 2027 budget, aims to save $15 million annually by cutting weight-loss drug costs.
Affected drugs will remain covered by MassHealth for other medical conditions like Type 2 diabetes.

title: Here’s the 7 prospects Bruins added on Day 2 of 2026 NHL Draft
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins selected seven players in Rounds 2-7 of the 2026 NHL Draft, including two goalies.
Yuri Ivanov, a goalie from MHK Spartak Moskva, was the Bruins' first pick at No. 56 overall.
The Bruins added winger Matvei Kotkov and defenseman Jacob Vandeven among their Day 2 selections.

title: Native Venezuelan Willson Contreras speaks on devastating earthquakes: ‘I always keep Venezuela in my heart’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Two earthquakes in Venezuela resulted in at least 1,430 deaths and 68,900 people missing.
Red Sox players, including Willson Contreras, show support with messages on their hats for Venezuela.
David Ortiz raises awareness and funds at Fenway Park for Venezuela earthquake relief efforts.

title: Jaylen Brown responds to report that he’d be the 7th-best player on an NBA team according to analytics
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jaylen Brown criticized analytics after a report labeled him as the seventh-best player on an NBA team.
Brown is coming off a season where he finished in the top-10 of MVP voting and solidified his NBA status.
NBA insider Bobby Marks's comments on analytics stirred debate, with notable figures defending Brown's performance.

title: Red Sox jump on Gerrit Cole early to defeat Yankees in third straight game
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox defeated Yankees 4-1, marking a third consecutive win in the series.
Gerrit Cole allowed four runs, raising his career ERA against Boston above six.
Jake Bennett pitched 6.1 innings, allowing three hits and earning his second win.

title: Bruins trade Fabian Lysell to Colorado for forward Ivan Ivan
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins traded winger Fabian Lysell to the Colorado Avalanche for forward Ivan Ivan on June 27, 2026.
Fabian Lysell, a 2021 first-round pick, played 12 NHL games for the Bruins with one goal and two assists.
Ivan Ivan, 23, scored 26 points in 66 AHL games and has appeared in 49 NHL games with the Avalanche.

title: Forget the celebrities in the crowd, France are the entertainers of the 2026 World Cup
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: France defeated Norway 4-1 in a World Cup match at Gillette Stadium, Boston.
French players, led by Kylian Mbappe, showcased impressive skill and teamwork throughout the game.
Ousmane Dembélé scored quickly, reinforcing France's reputation as World Cup favorites.

title: Cape DA investigating alleged threats at Sandwich Middle High
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Sandwich police and Cape & Islands DA investigating specific threat by student at Sandwich Middle High School.
Threat detected by GoGuardian app; flagged language on school-issued device alerted administrators and police.
Student involved in the threat not attending school; investigation ongoing to ensure safety, say officials.
